name: Basic Sourdough Starter (with Potato)
recipeIngredient: 1 large baking potato, peeled and cubed
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the potato in water to cover until tender. Drain off the liquid and save the potato for other use.
2. Let the potato water cool to lukewarm.
3. In a glass or ceramic bowl that has been scalded, combine the flour, yeast, and sugar.
4. Add the lukewarm potato water and stir well until all ingredients are incorporated.
5. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and pierce with a fork to release gases.
6. Place the bowl in a warm, draft-free location at an even 85°F for 2 days, stirring several times daily.
7. Do not let the sourdough starter rise above 95°F, as higher temperatures favor less desirable microorganisms.
8. Refrigerate until ready to use. To replenish, add one cup of flour and three-quarters of a cup of water, then let stand overnight or for 12 hours in a warm location before refrigerating again.
9. When replenishing, add lukewarm water with flour to the starter.
10. The starter should be at room temperature when using in recipes, after having stood for 12 hours from the addition of replenishing flour and water.
11. At least one cup should remain in the refrigerator.
